title: Delicate Dehydrated Indian Cauliflower Aloo Gobi
description: A grassy, fluffy, monsoon Indian dish featuring egg roll wrappers, dehydrated to perfection, with an ayurvedic-rajasic friendly twist on the classic aloo gobi recipe.
instructions: Preheat the dehydrator to 135 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pan, heat the ghee over medium heat and saute the onion, garlic, and ginger until the onion is translucent.
Add the cumin seeds, turmeric, and salt to the pan and stir for 1 minute.
Add the cauliflower and potatoes to the pan and stir to combine with the spice mixture.
Arrange the egg roll wrappers on the dehydrator trays in a single layer.
Spoon the cauliflower and potato mixture onto the center of each egg roll wrapper, leaving a 1 inch border around the edges.
Fold the bottom edge of the wrapper over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the wrapper to form a tight cylinder.
Dehydrate the aloo gobi rolls for 6 hours, or until they are dry and slightly flexible.
Serve the dehydrated aloo gobi rolls warm, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.
